name: Feeding America
mission: Our mission is to advance change in America by ensuring equitable access to nutritious food for all in partnership with partner food banks, policymakers...
theoryOfChange: Feeding America has developed a 'Levels of Evidence Framework' to systematically assess and describe how well interventions work towards achieving specified outcomes, identify interventions needing further evaluation, develop a shared language for intervention effectiveness, and provide transparent information to food banks. This framework helps in understanding what works, what is ineffective, and where more evaluation is needed. Additionally, for their Ending Hunger Community of Practice, Feeding America utilized a logic model and measurement framework to evaluate various interventions. They also crafted a theory of change for their Equity Impact Fund to identify the root causes of food insecurity and develop evaluation practices.

scoreSummary: Feeding AmericaHQ shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: 97% of reported revenue is noncash (gifts-in-kind)
keyConcerns.detail: Noncash contributions inflate reported program ratios. Cash-adjusted metrics better reflect actual spending.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 22/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Build reserves toward your board-approved target range (often 3-12 months), adjusted for revenue volatility and obligations.
components.name: Financial Health

judge_issues.message: Evaluation source domain 'norc.org' is not a known trusted evaluator
Evaluation source domain 'mdpi.com' is not a known trusted evaluator
Website citation for 'claims_zakat_eligible' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: GIK inflation: 97% of revenue is noncash contributions

risks.description: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking
GIK inflation: 97% of revenue is noncash contributions

score_details.score_summary: Feeding AmericaHQ shows good impact and moderate alignment (significant risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://feedingamerica.org)

all_citations.claim: The charity holds a 96.33333333333333/100 score and maintains a 98.3% program expense ratio.
Total revenue for the period was $5,047,685,550.
The organization provides grants and assistance to domestic organizations to support food security.
The organization receives significant noncash food and grocery contributions to support its food rescue programs.
Feeding America manages large-scale grants and assistance to a nationwide network of food banks.
The organization accepts general charitable donations (sadaqah) to fund its mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score because while its financial efficiency and scale are exemplary, its focus on systemic advocacy and lower working capital reserves result in a balanced impact profile.
areas_for_improvement: Limited liquidity with working capital sustaining only 1.4 months of operations
High cost per beneficiary relative to direct-service models due to a focus on systemic change and advocacy

dimension_explanations.alignment: Feeding AmericaHQ is identified as sadaqah-eligible, offering a medium fit for Muslim donors seeking to support domestic hunger relief through established institutional pathways [8]. The organization's focus on advocacy aligns with urgent needs for long-term food security solutions in America [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates a high level of financial health and transparency, maintaining a 98.3% program expense ratio [1][2]. While it focuses on systemic change, the calculated cost per beneficiary is $21,764.88, reflecting its role in large-scale infrastructure and policy advocacy rather than just direct meal provision [5][7].

baseline_narrative.headline: Feeding AmericaHQ is a leading hunger-relief organization dedicated to ensuring equitable food access through a massive network of partner food banks and advocacy initiatives.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 98.3%, indicating the vast majority of funds directly support hunger relief
Highly efficient fundraising operations, spending only $0.01 to raise every $1
Strong financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 96.33333333333333/100
baseline_narrative.summary: Feeding AmericaHQ operates as a nationwide network focused on food access and food rescue to combat food insecurity in the United States [1][5]. The organization reported a total revenue of $5,047,685,550, driven largely by significant noncash food and grocery contributions [2][5].

all_citations.quote: Noncash food and grocery contributions received: 4,627,410,422

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score because while its financial efficiency and scale are exemplary, its focus on systemic advocacy and lower working capital reserves result in a balanced impact profile.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization operates with <cite id="1">1.4 months of working capital</cite>, which is considered lean for an institution of this size.
Approximately <cite id="5">97% of revenue is noncash contributions</cite>, which can create volatility if supply chains or corporate donation policies shift.

bbb_assessment.summary: Feeding AmericaHQ is an Accredited Charity meeting all standards for accountability.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's massive scale and established corporate partnerships provide a buffer against individual supply chain disruptions.
risk_factors: <cite id="5">High GIK (Gifts-in-Kind) concentration</cite> at 97% of revenue
<cite id="1">Lean working capital</cite> sustaining only 1.3 months of operations
case_against.summary: The primary concern is the <cite id="5">97% reliance on noncash contributions</cite>, which can inflate revenue figures and mask a relatively lean cash position of <cite id="1">1.4 months of working capital</cite>.

confidence.data_freshness: Data current through 2023 Tax Year and 2024 ratings
data_gaps: Employee and volunteer counts not explicitly listed in provided snippets
confidence.notes: Extremely high financial transparency and third-party verification.

known_gaps: Specific employee count not provided in summary data

alignment.explanation: It has a <cite id="2">36-year track record</cite> of service.
alignment.improvement: Expand services to <cite id="2">underserved populations or geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage to increase the 'Underserved Space' score.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="1">96.96.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator score</cite> and its status as a <cite id="1">4-star rated institution</cite> for ten consecutive evaluations. Governance is robust, featuring a <cite id="2">17-member board with 100% independence</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the organization could pursue more granular transparency regarding its <cite id="5">valuation of noncash food contributions</cite> to mitigate concerns about GIK inflation.

impact.explanation: The organization demonstrates <cite id="1">100% program efficiency</cite> and a <cite id="1">perfect 100 Impact beacon score</cite>. It successfully moved <cite id="5">$4.8 billion in aid</cite> through its network in 2023.
impact.improvement: Build reserves toward a <cite id="1">target range of 3-12 months</cite> to improve long-term financial resilience against revenue volatility.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade A - Verified outcomes and strong theory of change

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes food and financial resources to a nationwide network of 200 food banks and 60,000 agencies.

rich_narrative.headline: Feeding AmericaHQ is a leading hunger-relief organization dedicated to ensuring equitable food access through a massive network of partner food banks and advocacy initiatives.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support large-scale, highly efficient domestic hunger relief and value a charity that leverages corporate partnerships to maximize impact.
donor_motivations: Want to address food insecurity in the United States
Value extreme financial efficiency and low fundraising overhead
Prefer supporting established national institutions with high transparency ratings
Interested in systemic change and hunger research
giving_considerations: Donations support a massive network rather than a single local pantry
The organization is highly dependent on noncash food donations
Cash reserves are relatively low compared to annual throughput

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization has a <cite id="1">verified Impact score of 100/100</cite> from Charity Navigator and maintains a <cite id="1">strong, documented theory of change</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: Feeding AmericaHQ uses a systemic change model that combines food rescue, large-scale distribution through food banks, and policy advocacy to end hunger.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Rigorous evidence ensures that the billions of dollars in food and cash are effectively reducing food insecurity metrics.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Despite its multi-billion dollar scale, the organization maintains <cite id="1">lean reserves of 1.3 months</cite>, indicating a high capacity to absorb and deploy additional cash for operations.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by a <cite id="1">100.0% program ratio</cite>, significantly higher than the <cite id="1">peer median of 85.2%</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar focus on humanitarian relief and advocacy
similar_organizations.name: IMRC

similar_organizations.differentiator: Smaller scale advocacy and relief focus
similar_organizations.name: Rebuilding Alliance

similar_organizations.differentiator: Focus on advocacy and legal systemic change
similar_organizations.name: ACLU Foundation

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">98.3% program expense ratio</cite> and spends just <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite>, maximizing the utility of every dollar donated.
In 2023 alone, the charity distributed <cite id="5">$4,819,232,913 in grants and similar assistance</cite> to its network of domestic organizations.
Feeding AmericaHQ holds an <cite id="1">overall score of 96.96.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>, supported by a <cite id="1">100% independent board</cite> of 17 members.

strengths_deep_dive: Feeding AmericaHQ's strength lies in its ability to leverage massive corporate and agricultural surpluses. By processing <cite id="5">over $4.6 billion in noncash contributions</cite>, the organization acts as a high-volume clearinghouse for the nation's food banks. This scale is managed with high transparency, earning <cite id="1">perfect beacon scores of 100 in both Impact and Culture</cite> from Charity Navigator. Furthermore, its grantmaking capacity is significant, providing <cite id="2">$682,025,103 in total grants</cite> to 714 domestic recipients, including major regional hubs like <cite id="2">Second Harvest Heartland and City Harvest</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Feeding AmericaHQ is the nation's largest domestic hunger-relief organization, operating as a massive network of food banks and advocacy initiatives to ensure <cite id="1">equitable access to nutritious food</cite>. The organization manages an unprecedented scale of operations, reporting <cite id="2">$4,916,912,461 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. A defining characteristic of its financial model is the heavy reliance on in-kind support, with <cite id="5">$4,627,410,422 received in noncash food and grocery contributions</cite>. This model allows the charity to maintain an <cite id="1">exceptional program expense ratio of 98.3%</cite>, ensuring that resources are directed toward its core mission of food access and disaster response. Beyond direct food distribution, the organization invests heavily in <cite id="1">hunger research and systemic change</cite> to address the root causes of food insecurity across the United States and Puerto Rico. Its operational efficiency is further highlighted by its fundraising prowess, requiring only <cite id="1">$0.01 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable support. This lean administrative structure, combined with a <cite id="1">perfect 100 score in Impact</cite> from Charity Navigator, positions the organization as a central pillar of the American social safety net.
